message:
ui: Browse.refresh fires real rulake_list_collections in live mode

Iteration 14. Closes the loop iteration 13 opened — the new
rulake_list_collections tool is now wired through the Console's
Browse-screen Refresh button. Live mode replaces the setTimeout
fixture flow with a real tools/call over the Streamable HTTP
transport.

ui/src/components/screens.jsx — ConnectScreen.test()
  - On a successful initialize, pins window.RULakeActiveClient =
    { endpoint, mode, token, sessionId, client } and dispatches a
    rulake:live-connected CustomEvent. Other screens (Browse here,
    Audit/Bundle next) use the active client to dispatch follow-up
    tool calls without re-handshaking.

ui/src/components/screens.jsx — BrowseScreen.refresh()
  - Now async. If window.RULakeActiveClient exists, runs the live
    path: POST /tools/call rulake_list_backends to enumerate
    registered backends; for each, POST /tools/call rulake_list_collections
    to enumerate its collections. Drains the SSE response stream for
    the data-line carrying the JSON-RPC `result` envelope (same
    pattern Connect.test uses). Reads `result.structuredContent` ?
    `result.content` to handle both wrappings rmcp emits.
  - Caches the result in window.RULakeLiveCollections so the next
    iteration's screen render can swap fixture for live data.
  - Audit codes:
      LIST_COLLECTIONS_OK      ok       — wire round-trip + parsed
      LIST_COLLECTIONS_FAILED  refused  — network 4xx/5xx, parse error,
                                          missing result envelope
  - When no active client exists, falls through to the existing
    fixture-refresh setTimeout (demo mode preserved).

ui/scripts/smoke.sh — --live phase
  - New 7th action-chain step: navigate to Backends, click Refresh.
    Asserts the new LIST_COLLECTIONS_OK audit code.
  - Adds the mcp-server's anon-default fixture LocalBackend ("local"
    with zero collections) coverage — proves the wire shape works
    even when the backend is empty.

E2E validated this iteration:
    $ ./scripts/smoke.sh --live
      ▸ build                 ✓
      ▸ preview               ✓
      ▸ mcp-server (live)     ✓ (CORS preflight 204)
      ▸ browser session       ✓
      ▸ bootstrap             ✓
      ▸ action chain          ✓ × 7 (added Browse.refresh)
      ▸ audit ledger          ✓ × 7 codes:
          WITNESS_MATCH | IPFS_BUNDLE_VERIFIED | OK_VERIFIED_MAIN
          | IPFS_OK | CONNECT_FAILED | INIT_OK | LIST_COLLECTIONS_OK
      ▸ console errors        ✓
      ✓ smoke green

The Console's Browse screen now renders fixture data BUT a click on
Refresh dispatches a real wire call. Next iteration: read
window.RULakeLiveCollections in the BrowseScreen render path so the
table swaps to live rows on success.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
